stem: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One of the angles formed has a measure of (3x + 10)°, and its corresponding angle on the other parallel line has a measure of (5x − 12)°. What is the value of x?
rationale: The correct answer is 11. Corresponding angles formed when a transversal cuts two parallel lines are congruent and therefore have equal measures: 3x + 10 = 5x − 12. Solving for x: 22 = 2x, so x = 11.
